title: An Operational Approach to Eliminating Backlogs in the Social Security Disability Program
description: Policy paper (June 2019) by Gerald Ray and Glenn Sklar, a project of the Fiscal Institute / SSDI Solutions Initiative (McCrery-Pomeroy) at the Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget (CRFB). The 40-page paper explains why disability backlogs and processing delays recur in SSA's disability program (more than two million people file claims annually; surges in hearing requests and appeals cause backlogs) and offers operational ideas to eliminate them, including staffing, performance-management, and training measures to boost employee performance; decisional support tools to standardize case review; data analytics to uncover adjudication problems and enhance case screening; enhanced quality assurance to reduce rework and detect/prevent fraud; and policy structures to facilitate natural-language-processing and AI applications to case adjudication. Several ideas are noted as proven effective at the Appeals Council and hearing operations.
geographic_coverage: United States (federal — SSA disability program, national)

source.publisher: Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget
